observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor create an Inventory Committee
composed of adequate number of members to conduct the physical inventory in
three months or less and require the Inventory Committee to prepare and submit
the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the Audit Team at least 10 days before the
scheduled start of inventory taking activities in accordance with COA Circular
2020-006 for the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. MGSO-Designate to issue PCs and PARs to previously acquired PPE and
maintain proper and complete file of said documents; and
b. Municipal Accountant to exhaust all available records to provide detailed
description on PPE totaling P12,840,265.89 which has generic or no
description at all.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the balances of RPT and SET Receivables and its reciprocal deferred
accounts. Consequently, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the
account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Identify the correct accounts for which the negative balances should have been
classified and prepare the corresponding adjusting entries;
b. Exert effort in identifying the remaining unidentified payable balances; and
c. Send out confirmation letters to the payees of the long outstanding payables
as to the validity of the balances and to request the submission of the pertinent
documents to support the claims for processing and payment.

In the event that the payees are unable to support the claims with the necessary
documents, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund,
subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a
condition that appropriation should be made in the event that a claim arises in the
future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
review and reconcile the negative balances of the accounts Due to NGAs and Due
to LGUs. Consequently, prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the
account balances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that the reconciliation of net cash flows from operating activities to surplus
presented in the NFS is reconciled with the net cash flows from operating activities
presented in the SCF.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to see to it that the SCBAA or the Notes to FS includes the required
disclosure in order for the users to properly understand the financial statement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the required supporting document for evaluation and ensure that all claims against government funds are properly supported with complete documentation in compliance with Section 4 (6) of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant submitted to the Audit Team the required supporting document on July 7, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the classification of expenses used in classifying its programs/projects/activities in budgeting reflect the nature of the expenses.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer is now using the nature of the programs/projects/activities in classifying its programs/projects/activities so as to enable the Municipal Accountant to record the expenses to the proper expense accounts and achieve fair presentation of the expenses in the financial statements.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a) provide clear cut guidelines and timeline to complete the Municipality’s annual physical count; and b) constantly monitor the status of the activities to ensure completion and submission of the complete RPCPPE within the set deadline; We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) Municipal Accountant to exhaust all available records to identify the unsupported PPE and consequently compute and record the related accumulated depreciation from the date the asset is available for use; b) Municipal Accountant to transfer the completed projects under the Trust Fund to the General Fund and recognize the related accumulated depreciation; c) Municipal Accountant to recognize accumulated depreciation of PPE without depreciation under the GF costing P43,783,941.94; d) Municipal Accountant to recompute the adjusted amount of accumulated depreciation of the properties with negative net book value and net book value with less than the prescribed 5 percent of salvage value; e) Municipal Accountant to reclassify the cost of plastic chairs and mono block chairs to the appropriate PPE account; and f) MGSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their respective records.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor together with the Municipality’s Inventory Committee is working on completing its annual physical inventory count. The Municipal Mayor has provided clear cut guidelines and timeline to complete the annual physical count. The Municipal Accountant has already recognized the unsupported PPE and consequently recognized the related accumulated depreciation from the date the asset is available for use as evidenced by JEV No. 100-2021-12-04799. The Municipal Accountant has also transferred the completed projects from the Trust Fund to the General Fund and recognized the related accumulated depreciation as evidenced by JEV No. 100-2021-12-04816 and 04817, respectively. The Municipal Accountant recognized the accumulated depreciation of PPE without depreciation under the GF costing P43,783,941.94 as evidenced by JEV No. 100-2021-12-04799. The Municipal Accountant adjusted the amount of accumulated depreciation of the properties with negative net book value and net book value. The Municipal Accountant reclassified the cost of plastic chairs as evidenced by JEV No. 100-2021-01-01152. The MGSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant is coordinating to reconcile their respective records.
observations.reason:
Inventory count of the Municipality’s PPE is still on-going, hence reconciliation is also on-going.
See Observation No. 1, Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MGSO and the MGSO-Designate to expedite the completion of the necessary documents needed to dispose the unserviceable properties in order to facilitate the immediate disposal thereof in compliance with Section 79 of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 379 to 381 of R.A. No. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has constructed a warehouse and the MGSO-Designate stated that upon gathering all the said properties, he will start facilitating the disposal.
observations.reason: Due to lack of storage to facilitate disposal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Assessor to: a) write a follow-up letter to the Port Manager of the PFDA requesting for the necessary documents to enable the Municipality to start facilitating the subject untitled lot; and b) facilitate the titling of the five parcels of land to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor has sent a follow-up letter to the Port Manager of the PFDA requesting for the necessary documents dated May 20, 2021; however, no response was received as of May 10, 2022. The Municipal Assessor has communicated with various other government agencies in order to secure the title of the remaining untitled lots.
observations.reason: The response was not received as of May 10, 2022.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MGSO-Designate to: a) facilitate the transfer of registration of the ambulance to the Municipality’s name and consequently, update its registration; and b) ensure that all vehicles owned by the Municipality are registered with the LTO and supported with OR/CR under the name of the Municipality and a photocopy of which is carried inside the vehicle at all times while the original copies should be filed and updated in the Office of the MGSO for monitoring of its yearly registration.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO stated that they are still in the process of registering the said ambulance to the Municipality's name.
observations.reason: The process of registration is on-going.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to exert more efforts to locate and submit to the Audit Team the remaining unsubmitted DVs of CYs 2016 and 2017 and their supporting documents for Audit Team evaluation and verification. We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the bank statements received from the bank showing debits/credits are supported with copy of pertinent debit/credit memos, and immediately draw JEV to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the books of accounts to ensure reliability of cash balance. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor follow-up with the Philippine National Police the results or any developments as to the execution of the warrant of arrest of the former Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant are still locating some of the missing DVs and have been able to locate most of it with the exemption of the missing DVs stated in the AOM of the Special Audit Team. The Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant informed the Audit Team that they believed they located most of it due to the efforts made. The Municipal Mayor promised that they will communicate with the prosecutor the results or any developments as to the execution of the warrant of arrest of the former Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: The last development was that they will communicate with the prosecutor to seek the guidance on the next step to take to be able to close the case and subsequently cause the full implementation of this finding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to: a) deposit collections promptly and intact, thereby avoiding the risk of loss and/or misuse of undeposited collections; and b) record all collections and deposits in the cashbooks on a daily basis to facilitate deposit of the exact amount.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is already depositing her collections promptly and intact. The Municipal Treasurer has her records updated on a timely basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial Government of Quezon for the conduct of auction of the delinquent properties and make a follow-up from the Sangguniang Bayan on the proposed resolution of tax amnesty to increase collection of the Municipality’s RPT and SET Receivables to be utilized for its development programs and projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality communicated this matter with the Provincial Government of Quezon; however, to date, the Sangguniang Panlalawigan has yet to act on it.
observations.reason: To date, the Sangguniang Panlalawigan has yet to act on it.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) Municipal Treasurer to immediately liquidate his remaining outstanding CA of P8,900.00; and b) Municipal Accountant to ensure that all CAs are liquidated at the end of the year to avoid possible loss or misuse.
observations.managementAction: The outstanding CA of P8,900.00 has already been liquidated. All CAs in CY 2021 were liquidated within the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor have the Municipal Accountant submit the required supporting document for the request for write-off of dormant receivables in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 for verification and validation by the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has submitted the required supporting document for the request for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a) Municipal Treasurer to write a collection letter to the resigned employee and enforce collection of the outstanding P26,748.39; b) Municipal Accountant to reclassify the balance of P26,748.39 to Other Receivables account; and c) Municipal Accountant to ensure that the amount of P4,000.00 be deducted from the terminal pay of the MHO.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer stated that they are still in the process of collecting from the resigned employee. The Municipal Accountant reclassified the Loans Receivable – Others account for the refund of PEI disallowance amounting to P9,082.00 and recorded it in the Due from Officers and Employees account. The Municipal Accountant ensured that the amount of P4,000.00 be deducted from the terminal pay of the MHO.
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer stated that they are still in the process of collecting from the resigned employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the MGSO-Designate submit to the Office of the Municipal Accountant a copy of the RPCI with certification that all prior years’ inventory balances were already issued accordingly. Consequently, require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books of the Municipality based on the submitted RPCI by the MGSO-Designate to establish the correct balance of inventory as of December 31, 2020.
observations.managementAction: The Management has constructed a new warehouse and believed that once it is fully functional, the recommendations will be fully implemented. Per results of analysis and reconciliation, the Municipal Accountant was able to make an adjusting entry pertaining to those that has been already verified.
observations.reason: Inventory and reconciliation of balances between the MGSO-Designate and the Municipal Accountant are on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) Municipal Treasurer to adopt strict collection measures and strategies in collecting the outstanding loan balances of P142,141.12 from the retired/resigned employees in order for the Municipality to have additional funding for its livelihood projects; and b) Municipal Accountant to adjust the Loans Receivable – Others account for the refund of PEI disallowance amounting to P9,082.00 and record it in the Due from Officers and Employees account.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer was able to collect some of the loan balances from retired/resigned employees and has reduced its loan receivables to P129,059.12 as of December 31, 2021. The Municipal Accountant adjusted the Loans Receivable – Others account for the refund of PEI disallowance amounting to P9,082.00 and recorded it in the Due from Officers and Employees account.
observations.reason: Collection from retired/resigned employees is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist to: a) submit a quarterly report of biological assets showing the additions/deductions and changes in the fair value of the said biological assets; and b) provide the Office of the Municipal Accountant supporting documents, such as contracts or Memorandum of Agreement, on the ownership of the recorded Plants and Trees amounting to P232,720.00.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist has submitted to the Office of the Municipal Accountant the biological assets report including the Municipality’s recorded Plants and Trees as of December 31, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a) identify the correct accounts for which the negative balances should have been classified and prepare the corresponding adjusting entries; b) exert effort in identifying the remaining unidentified payable balances; and c) send out confirmation letters to the payees of the long outstanding payables as to the validity of the balances and to request the submission of the pertinent documents to support the claims for processing and payment. In the event that the payees are unable to support the claims with the necessary documents, revert the account to the Unappropriated Surplus of the General Fund, subject to an authority from the Sangguniang Bayan through a Resolution with a condition that appropriation should be made in the event that a claim arises in the future.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is amenable with the observation and recommendation and stated that she is still in the process of reconciling the accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of reconciling and analyzing the Accounts Payable balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor execute a new Contract of Lease with the market stallholders in accordance with the approved Market Code to protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Administrator stated that they have already facilitated the lease contracts of approximately 50 percent of the market stallholders in accordance with the approved Market Code to protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.reason: 50 percent of the lease contracts is still being facilitated.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the SCBAA or the Notes to FS includes the required disclosure in order for the users to properly understand the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: No action was taken by the Municipal Accountant. The Municipal Accountant stated that the reason for the non-compliance of the said report was the lack of manpower in her department, particularly those with technical expertise to help her analyze the transactions. As a solution, the Municipal Mayor stated that they have already created a plantilla position to resolve the matter and will start the hiring process as soon as possible.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant stated that the reason for the non-compliance of the said report was the lack of manpower in her department, particularly those with technical expertise to help her analyze the transactions. As a solution, the Municipal Mayor stated that they have already created a plantilla position to resolve the matter and will start the hiring process as soon as possible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the receipt of the DRRMF received from the National Government totaling P3,210,347.00 by recording it under the Trust Liability – DRRMF account in the Trust Fund books in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 to fairly present the concerned accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reclassified the balance in the books of the Municipality recorded it under the Trust Liability – DRRMF in the Trust Fund books in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 to fairly present the concerned accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC to submit the utilization report of the 20 percent Development Fund to the Audit Team to enable the Audit Team to evaluate the effective utilization of the fund intended for its development projects.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC was only able to submit the report for CY 2020 on May 10, 2022 while the report for CY 2021 was submitted on May 11, 2022. Since the reports were submitted on May 10 and 11, 2022, the Audit Team was not able to evaluate the submitted reports as they concluded the Audit on May 6, 2022.
observations.reason: Due to the lapses of the MPDC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person: a) together with the Heads of the Implementing Units, to ensure that all programs, plans and activities are properly planned and that problems that may arise be considered in planning the PPAs intended to be implemented; and b) to regularly observe/monitor the effective implementation and utilization of GAD Funds for the benefit of its intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person ensured that all programs, plans and activities under the GAD Fund are properly planned. The GAD Focal Person regularly observed/monitored the effective implementation and utilization of GAD Funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a) Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate remittance of Due to BIR amounting to P488,779.76; b) Municipal Treasurer to refund the BIR and other balances as necessary; c) Municipal Treasurer to refund the Due to PhilHealth amounting to P18,000.00 to its respective beneficiaries; d) Municipal Accountant to gather all relevant documents for the reconciliation of the Inter-Agency Payables and make necessary adjustments; and e) Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger of the Inter-Agency Payables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of reconciling the accounts and stated that she is still in the process of reconciling the balances to ensure that the balance is correct prior to remittance to the BIR and making the necessary adjustments. The Municipal Treasurer has already been depositing the remittances as applicable. The Municipal Accountant is maintaining a subsidiary ledger of the Inter-Agency Payables.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of reconciling and analyzing the balances.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the SCBAA or the Notes to FS includes the required disclosure in order for the users to properly understand the FS.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to comply with the audit recommendation for the succeeding yearend financial reporting.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the adjustment for the stale checks amounting to P25,011.04; b. She mentioned that the letter of request stated that replies and confirmations will be sent to the Special Audit Team. On the other hand, the Municipal Accountant committed to comply with the audit recommendation to ensure that the bank statements received from the bank showing debits/credits are supported with copy of pertinent supporting documents and to immediately record all valid reconciling items.
observations.reason: The debit/credit memos were still not yet adjusted since they still could not identify its nature. The remaining un-submitted DVs could still not be located.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to: a. deposit collections of the Municipality promptly and intact, thereby avoiding the risk of loss and/or misuse of undeposited collections; and b. record all collections and deposits in the cashbooks on a daily basis to facilitate deposit of the exact amount.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer commented that sometimes the vehicle provided to her is not available, hence she could not deposit her collections regularly. As regards to the updating of cashbook, she stated that due to voluminous work load, especially when the Municipal Mayor is around, she could not focus on her work due to some instructions given which gives distraction to her. However, she will try her best to update the cashbook.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous work load and sometimes non-availability of vehicle provided, the deposit made was still not regular.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a statement of the reason/s for non-collection of RPT and SET Receivables and submit to the SB together with the complete list of taxpayers with delinquencies with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection provided in RA No. 7160 in order for the Municipality to have funds for its various development programs and projects for the benefit and livelihood growth of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer, in her letter dated May 28, 2021, stated that due to the underlying effect of the devastation of calamities and the current pandemic, they could not force the people to pay their duties while they are enduring damages from their properties. Thus, she mentioned that the uncertainty in the collections of taxes is very evident during these times. As part of their solution in addressing the situation, the Sangguniang Bayan of Guinayangan recently submitted the resolution to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan of Quezon for the proposal of Tax Amnesty for the Municipality. When the Municipal Secretary made a follow-up, it was gathered that it was pending in the committee level due to irregular session. However, they still hope that the Provincial Government of Quezon will be able to continue the auction of properties in the ensuing year which they believe is the most effective measure.
observations.reason: Due to the effect of the COVID-19 pandemic and devastation of typhoons in the Municipality. They focused on recovery; auction and tax amnesty initiatives were delayed.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Accountant to withhold the salary of the Accountable Officers to compel them to settle their unliquidated CAs totaling P328,117.51; b. Municipal Accountant to refrain from granting additional cash advance unless the previous cash advance has been liquidated; and c. see to it that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of the year for fair presentation of the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer, in her letter dated May 19, 2021 stated that upon their review of their documents, the cash advance of P8,900.00 was granted May 16, 2017 representing payroll of the Municipality’s project and by following COA rules and regulations, it was immediately liquidated and transmitted to the Office of the Municipal Accountant on the same month evidenced by their logbook received by the accounting office. On the other hand, the Municipal Accountant stated that the liquidation documents are included in the inventory of missing vouchers. Nevertheless, she committed to comply with the audit recommendation to ensure that all CAs are liquidated at the end of the year.
observations.reason: CAs of P305,717.51 were already liquidated. CAs decreased to P55,400.00 due to lapses in preparing liquidation reports. The remaining unliquidated CAs decreased but still exist due to voluminous work load and missing supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. file the request for write-off with the Commission on Audit (COA) for the Due to GOCCs and Other Receivables accounts amounting to P1,160.73 and P90,394.66, respectively, along with the required supporting documents in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005; and b. exert efforts in identifying and locating the whereabouts of the loan beneficiaries recorded under the Due from NGOs/POs account totaling P95,000.00 and consequently enforce collection of the said loans.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant stated that they could not find the supporting documents and is probably washed out by the typhoon in the old building of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Lack of required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MGSO-Designate to: a. prepare and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team within the prescribed period; b. prepare and submit SSMI to the Designated Accounting Office on a regular basis in order for the Accounting Office to record the actual issuance; and c. comply strictly with the regulations/procedures on the proper management of inventories in order to have reliable balances of inventories and its corresponding expenses and to avoid loss, improper utilization and wastage of the same. We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor provide the Office of the MGSO stock room to facilitate monitoring of the proper receipt and disposal of inventories.
observations.managementAction: The MGSO-Designate stated, in his letter dated June 24, 2021, that he will submit to the Municipal Accountant a copy of the RPCI with certification that all prior year inventory balances were already issued. The MGSO-Designate mentioned that they are already submitting the SSMI to the Accounting Office on a regular basis starting the second half of CY 2020. The Municipal stock room is already in process of construction. Construction of the Municipal stock room is already in process.
observations.reason: Annual physical count is not yet completed; Documentary requirements and full reconciliation outstanding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the immediate refund of the outstanding loan balances of P160,369.12 in order for the Municipality to have additional funding for livelihood projects.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer wrote a collection letter in CY 2020, of the said amount, P18,228.00 was collected. She stated they are continuously exerting effort to collect despite the current crisis. The Municipal Accountant stated that adjustments will be made.
observations.reason: Lack of strict enforcement of collection.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist to: a. complete the conduct of the physical count/inventory of the animals and prepare and render the report thereon complete with supporting documents as proof of the status/condition of the animals; b. locate the remaining Memorandum of Agreement/Contract pertaining to the grant of breeding animals; c. provide the Office of the Municipal Accountant supporting documents on the ownership of the recorded Plants and Trees amounting to P232,720.00; and d. prepare a quarterly report on the status and progress of the Animal Dispersal Program and AMIA Project to assess its sustainability. We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger on the balance of the Municipality’s Biological Asset for proper monitoring and accountability.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Agriculturist prepared and submitted the physical inventory of Biological Assets as of December 31, 2020 and located the Memorandum of Agreement. The Municipal Accountant committed to maintain subsidiary ledger subject to submission of documents from the Municipal Agriculturist.
observations.reason: No supporting documents for the status/condition of the animals (e.g., pictures/newborn documentation) were attached; progress report on Animal Dispersal Program not provided; voluminous work load.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger for Accounts Payable and to provide the details or breakdown of the account to ensure its accuracy and validity. Also to identify the correct accounts for which the negative balances should have been classified and prepare the corresponding adjusting entries; exert effort in identifying the remaining unidentified payable balances; and send out confirmation letters to the payees of the long outstanding payables as to the validity of the claims and to request them to submit the pertinent supporting documents for processing and payment.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared and submitted to the Audit Team the subsidiary ledger of the Accounts Payable. She committed to perform analysis of negative balances and to send confirmation letters after analysis.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor execute a new Contract of Lease with the market stallholders in accordance with the approved Market Code to protect the interest of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The New Market Code was approved by the Sangguniang Bayan on September 21, 2020. The Market Administrator-Designate stated that the Contract of Lease has been distributed to the respective stallholders and the accomplished contracts were already forwarded to the Designated Business Processing and Licensing Officer for review and appropriate action.
observations.reason: No ample time was available to implement the new Contract of Lease given the delayed approval of the New Market Code and the pandemic; hence implementation was hindered.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to submit the monthly, quarterly and annual FS/reports to facilitate timely audit action; and provide additional personnel in the Accounting Office.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant prepared the periodical FS/reports. Additional personnel was assigned to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRM Officer to maximize the utilization of LDRRMF by proper planning and full implementation of the LDRRMIP as mandated by law and to instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide the full appropriation of not less than five percent of the estimated revenue from regular sources to be set aside as LDRRMF to lessen the socio-economic and environmental impacts of disasters in the community and projected climate risks, and to enhance disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the community.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality maximized the utilization of their LDRRMF for CY 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to revert back the cash corresponding to the unutilized CY 2014 LDRRMF balance totaling P1,620,745.20 to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002 to avoid dormancy of funds which could have been appropriated and utilized for the Municipality’s social services programs.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer reverted back the cash corresponding to the utilized CY LDRRMF to the Unappropriated Surplus of the GF in CY 2020 in compliance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MPDC to maximize the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund so that all its programmed development projects could be implemented immediately to ensure that intended beneficiaries avail of the maximum benefits that will be derived therefrom. Also, instruct the MPDC in coordination with the Office of the Municipal Engineer to observe timeliness/proper planning so that projects programmed for the year are fully implemented.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) submitted the utilization report for CY 2020 on June 15, 2021. No timely submission hindered Audit Team evaluation.
observations.reason: No report was submitted by the MPDC in time; delayed submission hindered assessment of funds utilization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Treasurer to cause the immediate remittance of Due to BIR and GSIS; b. Municipal Accountant to gather all relevant documents for the reconciliation of the aforementioned Inter-Agency Payables and make necessary adjustments, as needed; and c. Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger of the Inter-Agency Payables.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant committed to comply with the audit recommendation and stated that account analysis is already in process.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous work load reconciliation of Due to BIR, GSIS and HDMF is still ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. provide clear-cut guidelines and timeline to complete the Municipality’s annual
physical count;
b. constantly monitor the status of the activities to ensure completion and
submission of the complete RPCPPE within the set deadline;
c. require the MGSO-Designate to: 1) ensure that all PPE counted by the Inventory
Committee are issued with PAR and provided with PC which should be
regularly updated; and 2) stick with property tag all counted PPE and ensure
to stick it to all newly acquired PPE before its release;
d. require the Municipal Accountant to: 1) exhaust all available records to identify
the unsupported PPE amounting to P175,880,265.77 and consequently
compute and record the related accumulated depreciation; and 2) re-compute
the amount of accumulated depreciation of the properties with negative net
book value; and
e. require the MGSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their
respective records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MGSO-Designate to attend
seminars/trainings and/or familiarize himself on the necessary guidelines on the
disposal of Municipal properties and henceforth, perform the essential activities to
facilitate said disposal.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Assessor to:
a. write a letter to the Port Manager of the PFDA requesting for the necessary
documents to enable the Municipality to start facilitating the subject untitled lot; and
b. facilitate the titling of the five parcels of land to avoid contingent claims that may
arise in the future.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MGSO-Designate to ensure that
all vehicles owned by the Municipality are registered with the LTO and supported with
OR/CR under the name of the Municipality and a photocopy of which is carried inside
the vehicle at all times. The original copies shall be filed and updated in the Office of
the MGSO for monitoring of its yearly registration.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record the
adjustment for the stale checks of P25,011.04;
b. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to make a constant follow-up
of their request to LBP regarding the particulars of various debit/credit
memos and consequently effect the adjustment immediately; and
c. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to exert more efforts to locate
and submit to the Audit Team the remaining unsubmitted DVs of CYs 2016
and 2017 and their supporting documents for evaluation and verification.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to see to it that the bank statements received from the bank showing
debits/credits are supported with copy of pertinent debit/credit memos, and immediately
draw JEV to record all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in
the books of accounts to ensure reliability of cash balance.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to:
a) deposit collections of the Municipality promptly and intact, thereby
avoiding the risk of loss and/or misuse of the undeposited collections; and
b) record all collections and deposits in the cashbooks on a daily basis to
facilitate deposit of the exact amount.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to prepare a statement of the reason/s for non-collection of RPT and SET
Receivables and submit to the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) together with the complete list
of taxpayers with delinquencies with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the
remedies for collection provided in Republic Act (RA) No. 7160 in order for the
Municipality to have funds for its various development programs and projects for the
benefit and livelihood growth of its constituents.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a. withhold the salary of the Accountable Officers to compel them to settle their
unliquidated CAs totaling P328,117.51;
b. refrain from granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash
advance has been liquidated; and
c. see to it that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of the year for fair
presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. file the request for write-off with the Commission on Audit (COA) the Due to
GOCCs and Other Receivables amounting to P1,160.73 and P90,394.66,
respectively, along with the required supporting documents in accordance with
COA Circular No. 2016-005; and
b. exert efforts in identifying and locating the whereabouts of the loan
beneficiaries recorded under the Due from NGOs/POs account totaling
P95,000.00 and consequently enforce collection of the said loans.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MGSO-Designate to:
a. prepare and submit the RPCI to the Audit Team within the prescribed period;
b. prepare and submit SSMI to the Accounting Office on a regular basis in order
for the Accounting Office to record the actual issuance of inventories; and
c. comply strictly with the regulations/procedures on the proper management
of inventories in order to have reliable balances of inventories and its
corresponding expenses and to avoid loss, improper utilization and wastage
of the same.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor provide the Office of
the MGSO stock room to facilitate monitoring of the proper receipt and disposal of
inventories.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the immediate refund of the outstanding loan
balances of P160,369.12 in order for the Municipality to have additional funding for
livelihood projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Agriculturist to:
a. complete the conduct of the physical count/inventory of the animals and
prepare and render the report thereon complete with supporting documents
as proof of the status/condition of the animals;
b. locate the remaining Memorandum of Agreement/Contracts pertaining to the
grant of breeding animals; and
c. provide the Office of the Municipal Accountant supporting documents on the
ownership of the recorded Plants and Trees amounting to P232,720.00; and
d. prepare a quarterly report on the status and progress of the Animal Dispersal
Program and AMIA Project to assess its sustainability.
We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to maintain a subsidiary ledger on the balance of the Municipality’s
Biological Asset for proper monitoring and accountability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare and maintain subsidiary ledger for Accounts Payable and to provide the
details or breakdown of the account to ensure its accuracy and validity.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor execute a new Contract of
Lease with the market stallholders to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
a. require the Municipal Accountant to submit on time the monthly, quarterly and
annual FS/reports to facilitate timely audit action; and
b. provide additional personnel in the Accounting Office.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to see to it that the SCBAA or the NFS includes the required disclosure in
order for the users to properly understand the FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-MT to prepare two separate reports for delinquencies, one for 2002 and above and another for 2001 and below and submit the same to the ICO-Municipal Accountant; ICO-Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustment of RPT and SET Receivables upon receipt of the correct report from ICO-MT to reflect the correct balance of these receivable accounts in the FS; ICO-Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger for each taxpayer; ICO-Municipal Accountant to set-up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of each year; and ICO-Municipal Accountant to make adjustment for the penalties by debiting the RPT and SET Receivables and crediting Prior Period Adjustments.
observations.managementAction: Provided the loan balance as of year-end. The ICO-MT and MT are coordinating for cleansing of accounts. ICO-MT to prepare the list of delinquents. ICO-Municipal Accountant to make adjustments once list is finalized.
observations.reason: The report submitted was consolidated. Set-up was also not made in 2018. Maintenance of SLs will be made once finalization of 2018 revision is made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: a) former Municipal Accountant to refund the P44,428.72 as liquidated damages; and b) ICO-Municipal Accountant to ensure that liquidated damages, if any, are duly recognized and deducted before payments are made to suppliers on account of its procurement activities.
observations.managementAction: Required and actual delivery dates were not indicated in the Inspection and Acceptance Report; hence the liquidated damages could not be computed. For issuance of Notice of Disallowance (ND).
observations.reason: Required and supporting documents were incomplete and the liquidated damages could not be computed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MDRRMO-Designate to submit to the Audit Team the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and the LDC in compliance with Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Committed to submit the monthly reports. The MDRRMO-Designate furnished to the Audit Team a copy of Resolution 2018-085(B) which approved the CY 2018 DRRMF-AIP for unexpended balances in CYs 2016 and 2017.
observations.reason: Not aware that a monthly report is being required by COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 in relation to the documentary requirements that shall be followed to substantiate the procurement activities of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman committed to comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason: Incomplete supporting documents and deficiencies were noted in the procurements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to review the legal, technical, and financial capabilities of suppliers, whether through Public Bidding or in the Alternative Modes of Procurement, to assure that the contract awardee is a bona fide supplier of the items to be purchased.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman committed to comply with the recommendations.
observations.reason: Incomplete supporting documents and deficiencies were noted in the procurements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-MT to comply strictly with the regulation by properly depositing all collections promptly and intact. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-MT to record all collections and deposits in the cashbooks on a daily basis; ICO-MT to rule, foot and certify the cashbooks at the end of each month; and ICO-Municipal Accountant and ICO-MT to reconcile the GL and cashbooks on a monthly basis.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor will request authority to maintain depository account in QCRB Guinayangan Branch to facilitate daily deposit of municipal collections.
observations.reason: Voluminous work.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MBO to provide budget for the repairs and maintenance and insurance of buildings and equipment used in the operations of Municipal Economic Enterprises for proper charging and reporting of expenses and for fair presentation of FS.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated under Audit Observation No. 8, Page 50.
observations.reason: Expenses related to economic enterprises were charged to GF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the ICO-Municipal Accountant and MBO to submit on time the monthly, quarterly and annual FSs/reports to facilitate timely audit action.
observations.managementAction: Required to submit FS on time. The Municipal Mayor committed to provide additional personnel to Accounting Office.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel and voluminous work.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Issue an office order creating the new Inventory Committee and Appraisal
Committee who will conduct the actual physical count of the Municipality’s
property and will appraise the PPE which has no available information
regarding its acquisition date and cost, respectively. The office order should
indicate the timeframe for these activities;
b) Constantly monitor the status of these activities to ensure completion and
submission of complete RPCPPE within the set deadline;
c) Require the accountable officer to pay the cost of PPE which were found
missing and has no request for relief from accountability, if any;
d) Require the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO)-Designate to ensure that all
PPE counted by the Inventory Committee are issued with PAR and provided with PC
which should be regularly updated;
e) Require the MGSO-Designate to require the signature of the accountable
officer on all PARs as proof of receipt of PPE;
f) Require the MGSO-Designate to stick with property tag all counted PPE and
ensure to stick it to all newly acquired PPE before its release;
g) Require the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjustments for PPE
and other affected accounts to record the PPE acquired under SEF and TF
totaling P261,550.00;
h) Require the MGSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to exert efforts to
identify/locate the unaccounted PPE totaling P12,092,338.65; and
i) Require the MGSO-Designate and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their
records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to
record the adjustment for the new stale checks;
b) Require the MT to constantly follow-up her request to LBP regarding the
particulars of various debit/credit memos;
c) Require the Municipal Accountant and MT to exert more efforts to locate and
submit to the Audit Team the remaining DVs of CYs 2016 and 2017 and its
supporting documents; and
d) Require the Municipal Accountant to exert effort to account and make proper
adjustment on the unaccounted amount of P148,095.54.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Accountant to see to it that the bank statements received from the bank
showing debits/credits are supported with copy of pertinent debit/credit memos,
and immediately draw JEV to record all valid reconciling items that require
adjustment and correction in the books of accounts to ensure reliability of cash
balance.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) MT to prepare a list of individual taxpayers with delinquencies for CY 2001
and below and another list for CYs 2002 to 2018 as of December 31, 2018 and
furnish it to the Municipal Accountant; and
b) Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments of RPT/SET
Receivables once the list of delinquent taxpayers for CYs 2002 to 2018 is
received from the MT to reflect the correct balance of these receivable
accounts.
c) Municipal Accountant to set-up the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning
of each year; and
d) Municipal Accountant to maintain subsidiary ledger for each taxpayer to
facilitate monitoring and preparation of necessary reports/schedules;
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MT to
prepare a statement of the reason/s for such non-collection or non-payment and
submit to the SB together with the complete list of taxpayers with delinquencies
with a request for assistance in the enforcement of the remedies for collection
provided in RA No. 7160 in order for the Municipality to have funds for other
projects that will benefit its constituents.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Assessor and Municipal Accountant to submit to the Audit Team the documents
that served as their basis of valuation of 15 parcels of land for the latter’s
evaluation.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the
Municipal Assessor to prioritize the titling of the five parcels of land in the name of
the Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MGSO-
Designate and Municipal Accountant to comply strictly with the
regulations/procedures on the proper accounting, recording and management of
inventories in accordance with the provisions of MNGAS for LGUs in order to have
reliable balances of inventories and its corresponding expenses and to avoid loss,
improper utilization and wastage of the same.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor provide the
Office of MGSO with stock room to facilitate compliance with the regulation on
proper receipt and disposal of inventories.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Agriculturist to:
a) Locate the remaining Memorandum of Agreement/Contracts pertaining to the
grant of breeding animals;
b) Complete the conduct of the physical count/inventory of the animals and
prepare and render the report thereon complete with supporting documents as
proof of the status/condition of animals; and
c) Conduct a complete investigation on the circumstances surrounding the death
of the animals and if negligence was committed, compel the concerned
recipients to refund the cost of the animals.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
adjust the books of account by debiting the Prior Year’s Adjustments and
crediting the Advances for Operating Expenses and Advances to Payroll accounts
amounting to P244,706.21 and P88,860.86, respectively for fair presentation in the
financial statements.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a) Submit to the Audit Team the liquidation reports for CAs totaling
P1,641,287.98 for review and evaluation;
b) Record the liquidation of any CA only if it is supported with Liquidation
Report and supporting documents for fair presentation of accounts in the
FS; and
c) Withhold the salary of the Accountable Officers to compel them to settle their
unliquidated CAs totaling P152,693.00.
Henceforth, ensure that all CAs are fully liquidated at the end of each year in
compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer
(MBO) to comply with the regulations regarding the proper budgeting of expenses
for economic enterprises for fair presentation of accounts in the financial
statements.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to see to it that the SCBAA or the Notes to FS includes the required
disclosure in order for the users to properly understand the FS.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the incumbent Municipal
Accountant in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer to exert more efforts to
locate and submit to the Audit Team the remaining 534 DVs and their supporting
documents for audit.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor make constant follow up to the
Office of the Provincial Prosecutor on his complaint against the former Municipal
Accountant regarding the unsubmitted DVs to hasten the release of Court
Decision on the case.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to facilitate the immediate refund of the
outstanding loan balance of P1,473,465.18 in order for the Municipality to have
funds for its priority projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
a) Municipal Accountant and BAC Chairman to submit the lacking documents to
the Audit Team for evaluation; and
b) Concerned offices to accomplish completely and properly the accounting forms
related to procurements.
We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC
to strictly comply with the provisions of the 2016 IRR of RA No. 9184 in relation to
the documentary requirements to substantiate the procurement activities of the
Municipality.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MT to:
a) Deposit her collections promptly and intact, thereby avoiding the risk of loss
and/or misuse of undeposited collections; and
b) Record all collections and deposits in the cashbooks on a daily basis to
facilitate deposit of exact amount.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor execute a new Contract of Lease with the
market stallholders to protect the interest of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the Municipal Accountant to submit on time the monthly, quarterly and
annual FS/reports to facilitate timely audit action; and
b) Provide additional personnel in the Accounting Office.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to maintain complete sets of subsidiary ledgers for all economic
enterprises operated by the Municipality to facilitate monitoring and preparation of
the required FS and schedules.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a) Municipal Engineer and other concerned implementing offices to submit the
list of all on-going government PPAs of the immediately preceding year;
b) Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to submit the list of
PPAs that are to be implemented during the year under review;
c) Municipal Engineer and other concerned implementing offices to inform the
Audit Team within ten days after the award of the project or before the start of
the program/activity that the appropriate project signboards and/or public
notices are already posted to facilitate validation; and
d) Municipal Engineer and other concerned implementing offices to submit the
monthly monitoring report to the Audit Team.

Municipal Accountant to see to it that the SCBAA or the Notes to FS includes

of actual amounts on a comparable basis to the budget and actual amounts in
the FS, in compliance with Paragraphs 14, 29 and 47 of PPSAS 24, to achieve

Treasurer to strictly comply with the requirements stated under COA Circular

and bear the approval and certification of the proper officials.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the ICO-Municipal
Treasurer to comply strictly with the above-mentioned regulation by properly
depositing all collections promptly and intact and monitoring her collections due
for deposits.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

breakdown of telephone expenses and facilitate the withholding of
creditable income tax and final value added tax (VAT) on GMP from
the service providers.
b. formulate and implement internal policies on landline and mobile
telephone utilization that will promote sound management and fiscal
administration towards efficient and economic use of limited
resources.

Municipal Accountant to see to it that the DVs are supported with complete

recorded in their proper accounts. Likewise, require the submission of the 14

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

their own MRF and to implement waste segregation at source;
b. strictly enforce and intensify the information campaign on the no-
littering policy among the residents; and
c. strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines
under RA No. 9003.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and MBO to henceforth submit on time the monthly, quarterly and
annual financial statements/reports to facilitate timely audit action.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to maintain complete sets of subsidiary ledgers for all economic
enterprises operated by the Municipality including the 20% DF to facilitate
monitoring and preparation of the required FS and schedules.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GSO-Designate

for each motor vehicle which should be supported with driver’s trip tickets

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Chairman
and the GSO-Designate to:
a. furnish the Audit Team with copies of Purchase Orders and Contracts
together with the supporting documents within the prescribed period
of five (5) days from the date of its issuance to afford the Auditor time

b. notify the Audit Team not later than 24 hours from acceptance of
deliveries for timely inspection.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Engineer and Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to
submit the listing of all-on-going government projects/programs/activities and
those that are for implementation during the year, as well as information that

the appropriate signboards and/or public notices have been already posted in

b. refund the amount of P609,730.33.

medicines not listed in the PNF. Also, refund the amount of P207,010.08

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to ensure
